title: Executive Order 8952—Revoking Executive Order No. 8458 of June 27, 1940, as Amended by Executive Order No. 8532 of September 4, 1940, and Authorizing Persons Appointed from the Emergency Replacement List Established Pursuant to Such Orders to Acquire a Classified Civil Service Status

flags.justification: A narrow, bounded, criteria-based conversion mechanism for already-serving employees.
Specific, verifiable conditions: appointment and duty-entry deadlines and a minimum six-month continuous-service requirement.
This defines eligibility criteria for a status conversion, not an enforcement regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

This properly winds down the underlying emergency mechanism (effective December 31, 1941) while creating a bounded conversion pathway for those already on the list — a termination-plus-transition action, not an open-ended authority grant.

This properly winds down an emergency personnel measure by converting affected employees to regular civil-service status under normal statutory provisions, a regularizing rather than oversight-removing action.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A routine personnel-regularization action, similar in character to EO 8457.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
Bounded conversion criteria for already-serving employees, not an ongoing exemption pathway.
